---
title: "rig library"
---

<!-- Generated by website/gen-cli-reference.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

## `rig library`

```text
Description:
  Manage package libraries [alias: lib] (experimental)

  rig supports multiple user package libraries. The usual user library is
  called "main".

  `rig library default` shows or sets the default library for the
   current R version.
  `rig library list` lists all libraries for the current R version.
  `rig library add` adds a new library for the current R version.
  `rig library rm` deletes a library, including all packages in it.
  It is not possible to delete the current default library, and it is not
  possible to delete the main library.

  User libraries are implemented at the user level, no administrator or
  root password is needed to add, set or delete them. If you delete an
  R installation, the user package libraries and their configurations are
  kept for all users on the system.

  `rig library` is currently experimental, and might change in future
  versions. Feedback is appreciated.

Usage: rig library [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

Commands:
  add      Add a new library
  default  Set the default library
  list     List libraries [alias: ls]
  rm       Remove a library
  help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --json
          JSON output

      --user
          Run in user mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)

      --admin
          Run in admin mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')
```

### `rig library add`

```text
Add a new library

Usage: rig library add [OPTIONS] <lib-name>

Arguments:
  <lib-name>  name of new library

Options:
      --user   Run in user mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
      --admin  Run in admin mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
  -h, --help   Print help
```

### `rig library default`

```text
Set the default library

Usage: rig library default [OPTIONS] [lib-name]

Arguments:
  [lib-name]  library name to set as default

Options:
      --json   JSON output
      --user   Run in user mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
      --admin  Run in admin mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
  -h, --help   Print help
```

### `rig library list`

```text
List libraries [alias: ls]

Usage: rig library list [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --json   JSON output
      --user   Run in user mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
      --admin  Run in admin mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
  -h, --help   Print help
```

### `rig library rm`

```text
Remove a library

Usage: rig library rm [OPTIONS] <lib-name>

Arguments:
  <lib-name>  name of library to remove

Options:
      --user   Run in user mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
      --admin  Run in admin mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)
  -h, --help   Print help
```
